exemplar.title: How does language reveal the social masks of the protagonists in William Shakespeareâs Hamlet and Luigi Pirandelloâs The Late Mattia Pascal?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does language reveal the social masks of the protagonists in William Shakespeareâs Hamlet and Luigi Pirandelloâs The Late Mattia Pascal?

subject.title: English Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Literature focuses exclusively on the study of literature, developing students' appreciation and critical analysis of literary works from various cultures, periods, and genres. Students examine poetry, prose, and drama through close reading, comparative analysis, and exploration of literary techniques and themes. The course cultivates sophisticated interpretive skills and deep understanding of how literature reflects and shapes human experience across different cultural contexts.

strengths: Title and opening paragraph communicate the investigationâs focus clearly and effectively
Research question is explicitly stated and revisited throughout to maintain focus
Headings and transitions sustain clear purpose and direction
weaknesses: Thesis statement could be phrased more assertively in active voice
Methodology lacks an explicit rationale for source selection
No discussion of source evaluation or reliability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission demonstrates accurate and effective communication of topic and focus, and the research question is tightly framed and consistently linked to discussion. The methodological framework is generally complete with a relevant range of sources, but the rationale for selecting specific secondary materials and an evaluation of their reliability are missing. These considerations place the work in the upper-middle band (5/6) rather than the top mark.

strengths: Accurate and frequent use of subject-specific terminology (e.g., paronomasia, epizeuxis)
Insightful contextualisation of medieval social norms enriches close readings
Precise citation of key editions (e.g., 2009 Oxford School Shakespeare)
weaknesses: Some assertions rely on general claims or plot summary rather than detailed evidence
Citation of Jungâs âPersonaâ lacks full bibliographic details
Cultural and historical context is only briefly addressed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows a coherent understanding of both primary texts and secondary sources, and employs disciplinary vocabulary with precision. However, knowledge sometimes remains at a general level and contextual/historical grounding is uneven. These strengths and limitations align with the 5/6 band for Knowledge and Understanding, meriting a score of 5.

strengths: Consistent use of primary passages and relevant critical voices to address the research question
Effective comparative analysis (Hamlet vs Pascal) that yields clear interpretive insights
Integration of scholarship (Davis, Sabio) enriches depth of argument
Conclusion synthesises comparative findings to reaffirm the central thesis
weaknesses: Coverage of some critical traditions (e.g., Pirandello) is thinner and lacks recent scholarship
Counter-arguments and critical evaluation of sources are limited
Extensive quotations sometimes overshadow original analysis
Conclusion restates rather than weighs alternative interpretations or limitations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work exhibits good to very good research quality, depth of analysis, and development of a coherent argument. While the analysis is mostly well supported and the discussion is focused, shortcomings in source diversity, critical evaluation, and full exploration of counter-arguments prevent placement in the top band. A high âgoodâ rating (9/12) reflects these considerations.

strengths: All required elements are present (word count, title, headings, pagination)
Report structure adheres to guidelines with clear signposting
Layout and formatting generally enhance readability
weaknesses: Occasional broken URLs and inconsistent dash/ellipsis spacing
Table of contents omits a page number and shows inconsistent entry formatting
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets all structural and formatting requirements and falls squarely within the highest band for Presentation (4/4). Minor layout inconsistencies and typographical issues do not significantly impede clarity or navigation.

strandSelections.reasoning: Primary sources are the two set texts; secondary support includes ten critical / theoretical references.  Citations are integrated, and a works-cited list is provided.  However, the essay does not explain the selection process or evaluate source reliability, and the secondary material skews toward older scholarship with limited theoretical diversity.  The methodology is therefore âmostly completeâ but not demonstrably âappropriate range â¦ and effective selection,â warranting the upper-middle band.

Formatting is generally correct: word count, title, headings hierarchy, and spacing are clear.  Minor layout flaws (occasional broken URLs, inconsistent dash/ellipsis spacing) prevent a perfect score but do not seriously impede readability, thus a solid mark within the highest band is appropriate.

Research draws consistently on primary passages and a selection of critical voices (Bloom, Rosenberg, Prior, Davis, Sabio).  All material is relevant to the question of humour and masks.  Nonetheless, coverage is uneven (Pirandello criticism is thinner than Shakespeare) and lacks recent scholarship, preventing the âexcellent/consistentlyâ level of 10-12.  The lower end of the 7-9 band therefore fits.

The student states the topic (âthe role of language in revealing social masksâ) in the title and restates it clearly in the opening paragraph.  The introduction signposts the scope (humour as the linguistic lens; two primary texts) and ends with a succinct thesis that is reiterated in the conclusion.  Throughout, headings and transitions maintain the announced focus, so the reader is never in doubt about purpose or direction.  Minor lapses in phrasing (âThe worth of this investigationâ¦ which has been too often consideredâ¦â) do not impede overall clarity, hence the top-band allocation.

The essay maintains a coherent argument that humour exposes social masks, and each section concludes with a mini-evaluation.  However, evaluation of sources is brief, and the conclusion largely restates earlier points without weighing alternative interpretations or limitations.  This reflects âgood argument development with partially critical evaluationâ, aligning with mid-band 4-6.

Analytical paragraphs typically move from quotation to explanation to interpretive claim, showing a logical chain of reasoning.  Comparative insights (different cultural contexts; humour as coping mechanism vs. performance device) are well developed.  Still, some points are asserted rather than demonstrated (e.g., Jungian âpersonaâ links), and counter-arguments are limited.  The analysis is âgood/mostly supportedâ rather than exemplary, placing it at the lower edge of 7-9.

The report follows the prescribed structure: title page with required details, table of contents, clear section headings, introductionâbodyâconclusion format, and works-cited list.  Pagination and internal signposting appear consistent.  All required elements are present, so the top presentation band is warranted.

Close readings of key scenes (1.2, 3.2, 4.4 in Hamlet; opening and Monte Carlo chapters in Pascal) show solid familiarity, and secondary critics are used to extend claims.  Yet some assertions remain general (âhumour is primarily noticeableâ¦â) or rely on plot summary, and cultural / historical context is only briefly touched.  Knowledge is therefore good but not consistently âexcellentâ, meriting the 3-4 band.

The student deploys literary terminology accurately and frequently (paronomasia, epizeuxis, anacoluthon, illeism, conduplicatio).  Conceptual vocabulary from Jungian psychology and theatrical theory is also used with competence.  A handful of slips in punctuation or spacing do not affect conceptual accuracy, so a high score in the top band is justified, though occasional over-technical phrasing precludes a full 6.

The research question is explicitly formulated, sharply delimited to two named works and a single comparative angle (humour as revelatory language).  It is revisited at the start of each body section, showing sustained linkage to discussion.  A slightly compound phrasing (âHow does language revealâ¦â) leaves room for tighter refinement, so a strong but not perfect score within the highest band is appropriate.

annotations.comment: The precise deployment of terms like âparonomasiaâ and âepizeuxisâ demonstrates strong command of literary terminology.
The comparison between Hamlet and Pascal in the same paragraph is effective, providing clear juxtaposition of their humour strategies.
Math expressions for line references (e.g., (1.2.82)) appear in plain text; enclose them in $$â¦$$ for consistent formatting.
This paragraph spans two columns and over 450 words; consider splitting into smaller units to aid reader engagement.
The title precisely communicates the focus on language revealing social masks through comparative literary analysis, providing clear purpose and direction.
The research question is explicitly stated and tightly linked to the two primary texts, ensuring clarity of focus from the outset.
The inclusion of a word count demonstrates compliance with the IB requirement, confirming adherence to formatting guidelines.
The table of contents omits a page number for âMain Body,â disrupting navigability; ensure all entries have corresponding pagination.
Indentation and hierarchy in the table of contents vary between entries; apply consistent formatting to enhance presentation coherence.
The introductory sentence is lengthy and complex; consider splitting it into shorter sentences for readability and emphasis of key ideas.
The citation of Jungâs âPersonaâ lacks full bibliographic details; include publisher, year, and accessed date to strengthen source reliability.
A misplaced period interrupts the flow (âlanguage. whichâ); correct this punctuation to maintain clarity.
Revisiting the research question in the opening of the body section ensures sustained linkage and focus on the investigationâs purpose.
The thesis statement could be more assertive; rephrase with active voice (e.g., âThis essay argues that humour reveals masks byâ¦â).
Extensive quotation of Hamletâs lines sometimes overshadows your analysis; integrate more interpretive commentary between quotes.
The methodology applied to select critical sources is not discussed; include a brief rationale to demonstrate informed selection.
The clear âPlaying and Actingâ section heading effectively signals a shift in focus and aids reader navigation.
The term âDyonisianâ may be unfamiliar to some readers; define it briefly or include a footnote for clarity.
Effective integration of primary quotations in the discussion illustrates the revelatory function of humour.
Transition into âMasks and Crisesâ lacks a linking sentence summarising prior findings; add a bridge to maintain coherence.
Rosenbergâs theoretical insights are well cited; strengthen your argument by critically evaluating his interpretive stance.
Your scene 2.2 analysis demonstrates how humour reveals Hamletâs mask with well-supported examples.
There is inconsistency in citation formats (JSTOR entries vs. website URLs); standardise referencing style throughout.
The citation of the 2009 Oxford School Shakespeare edition is precise and supports your analysis with a stable reference.
The methodology selection is not addressed in the introduction; add a brief section explaining your choice of primary and secondary sources.
The Category label (âCategory: 2â) is unclear to the reader; clarify its meaning or remove if not required for academic context.
Insightful contextualisation of medieval social norms enriches your analysis of Hamletâs humour; this deepens understanding of the text.
The juxtaposition of Davis and Sabioâs perspectives enriches critical depth and shows strong engagement with scholarship.
The conclusion synthesises comparative insights effectively, reaffirming how humour acts as a revelatory lens.
The conclusion would benefit from acknowledging study limitations or suggesting avenues for further research.
